# Command

A searchable command list for quickly finding and running actions.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Filtering and selection](#filtering-and-selection)
- [Command dialog](#command-dialog)
- [State and data](#state-and-data)
- [Accessibility and composition](#accessibility-and-composition)

## Installation

Add the Command component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/command
```

## Usage

Use Command when users know what they want but the available actions or destinations are too numerous for a small menu. Give every `CommandItem` a stable, searchable `value`, group related results, and include `CommandEmpty` inside the list. Prefer a Select for choosing one value from a known form field and a Dropdown Menu for a short set of contextual actions.

**Command list example**

```vue
<template>
  <Command class="w-full max-w-md rounded-lg border shadow-md">
    <CommandInput placeholder="Type a command or search…" />
    <CommandList>
      <CommandEmpty>No results found.</CommandEmpty>
      <CommandGroup heading="Suggestions">
        <CommandItem value="Calendar">
          <CalendarIcon />
          <span>Calendar</span>
        </CommandItem>
        <CommandItem value="Search emoji">
          <ScanFaceIcon />
          <span>Search emoji</span>
        </CommandItem>
        <CommandItem value="Launch">
          <RocketIcon />
          <span>Launch</span>
        </CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
      <CommandSeparator />
      <CommandGroup heading="Settings">
        <CommandItem value="Profile">
          <FileUserIcon />
          <span>Profile</span>
          <CommandShortcut>⌘P</CommandShortcut>
        </CommandItem>
        <CommandItem value="Mail">
          <SendIcon />
          <span>Mail</span>
          <CommandShortcut>⌘B</CommandShortcut>
        </CommandItem>
        <CommandItem value="Settings">
          <SettingsIcon />
          <span>Settings</span>
          <CommandShortcut>⌘S</CommandShortcut>
        </CommandItem>
      </CommandGroup>
    </CommandList>
  </Command>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Command,
  CommandEmpty,
  CommandGroup,
  CommandInput,
  CommandItem,
  CommandList,
  CommandSeparator,
  CommandShortcut,
} from '@/Components/Command'
import {
  CalendarIcon,
  FileUserIcon,
  RocketIcon,
  ScanFaceIcon,
  SendIcon,
  SettingsIcon,
} from '@lucide/vue'
</script>
```

## Filtering and selection

`CommandInput` filters item text and values without case-sensitive matching. Write values that include useful search terms, but keep visible labels concise. Selecting an item clears the current query; handle the item's `select` event to run an action, navigate, or update application state.

```vue
<CommandItem
  value="Open project settings"
  @select="openProjectSettings"
>
  Project settings
</CommandItem>
```

Keep disabled results visible only when explaining why an action is unavailable helps the user. Otherwise omit them so search results stay relevant. Shortcut text in `CommandShortcut` is a hint, not a keyboard binding; register the actual shortcut separately.

## Command dialog

Use `CommandDialog` for a global command palette. Control it with `v-model:open`, supply a specific `title` and `description` for assistive technology, and let the dialog restore focus to the invoking control when it closes.

**Command dialog example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-3">
    <Button
      variant="outline"
      @click="open = true"
    >
      Open command menu
    </Button>
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      You can also press
      <kbd class="rounded border bg-muted px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-xs">
        ⌘/Ctrl J
      </kbd>
    </p>

    <CommandDialog
      v-model:open="open"
      title="Command menu"
      description="Search for a page or action."
    >
      <CommandInput placeholder="Type a command or search…" />
      <CommandList>
        <CommandEmpty>No results found.</CommandEmpty>
        <CommandGroup heading="Suggestions">
          <CommandItem value="calendar">Calendar</CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="search emoji">Search emoji</CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="calculator">Calculator</CommandItem>
        </CommandGroup>
        <CommandSeparator />
        <CommandGroup heading="Settings">
          <CommandItem value="profile">Profile</CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="billing">Billing</CommandItem>
          <CommandItem value="settings">Settings</CommandItem>
        </CommandGroup>
      </CommandList>
    </CommandDialog>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  CommandDialog,
  CommandEmpty,
  CommandGroup,
  CommandInput,
  CommandItem,
  CommandList,
  CommandSeparator,
} from '@/Components/Command'
import { onKeyStroke } from '@vueuse/core'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const open = ref(false)

onKeyStroke('j', (event) => {
  if (!event.metaKey && !event.ctrlKey) {
    return
  }

  event.preventDefault()
  open.value = !open.value
})
</script>
```

Choose a shortcut that does not override browser or operating-system behavior. Display both macOS and non-macOS forms when the application serves both, and always provide a visible button or menu entry as an alternative.

## State and data

The root forwards listbox value state, so use `v-model` when the selected value must be observed. Load remote results before rendering items or manage that request outside Command; the built-in filter only filters mounted items. Announce remote loading or failure near the list instead of presenting a misleading empty result.

## Accessibility and composition

`CommandInput` receives focus automatically. Arrow keys move through results, Enter selects the highlighted item, and Escape closes a surrounding dialog. Do not nest buttons or links inside an item; handle selection on the item so it remains one keyboard target.

If a custom item uses `as-child`, preserve the listbox semantics, forwarded events, and focus behavior described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

Hosted documentation: [Command](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/command)
